title: Negative Stain Transmission Electron Microscopy of ATG9A Vesicles
author: Elisabeth Holzer

steps.title: Glow discharge grids for 00:01:00 immediately...
steps.instruction: Glow discharge grids for 00:01:00 immediately before sample application to increase hydrophilicity.

steps.title: Apply purified ATG9A vesicle suspension to the glow-discharged grid
steps.instruction: Apply purified ATG9A vesicle suspension to the glow-discharged grid.

steps.title: Stain by applying 5 µL of...
steps.instruction: Stain by applying 5 µL of 2% uranyl acetate solution to the grid.

steps.title: Repeat the staining step once more...
steps.instruction: Repeat the staining step once more with fresh 2% uranyl acetate.

steps.title: Store dried grids under vacuum until imaging
steps.instruction: Store dried grids under vacuum until imaging.

steps.title: Image stained grids using a FEI...
steps.instruction: Image stained grids using a FEI Morgagni 268D transmission electron microscope equipped with a tungsten filament emitter , operated at 80 kV .

steps.title: Images were acquired at 44,000× magnification...
steps.instruction: Images were acquired at 44,000× magnification using an 11- megapixel Morada CCD camera (Olympus).

steps.title: Analyze acquired micrographs using ImageJ (RRID:SCR_003070; https://imagej
steps.instruction: Analyze acquired micrographs using ImageJ (RRID:SCR_003070; https://imagej.net/).
